description: Security input validation rules for detecting injection vulnerabilities, path traversal, XSS, and other input-related security issues
source: CWE (MITRE), SonarQube Security Rules (S3649, S5131, S2076, S2077, etc.), OWASP Top 10, CodeQL Security Queries

rules.name: SQL Injection via String Concatenation
rules.description: Detects SQL queries constructed using string concatenation or interpolation with user-controlled input, allowing attackers to manipulate query logic.
rules.detection_strategy:
Detect function calls to database query methods (execute, query, sql) where the query string argument contains binary_expression with operator::+ or macro_invocation format! that interpolates variables. AST query pattern:

```
(call_expression
  function: (field_expression field: (identifier) @field (#eq? @field "execute"))
  arguments: (arguments (binary_expression operator: (operator) @op (#eq? @op "+"))))
```

Alternative pattern for format! macro:
```
(macro_invocation macro: (identifier) @macro (#eq? @macro "format!")
  arguments: (token_tree) @args)
```

Preflight scan for: query, execute, sql, format!, format, diesel, sqlx, rusqlite
false_positive_guards: String is a constant/hardcoded SQL literal
Variable is a numeric primitive (not string-concatenated)
Input validated via sanitize function before use

vulnerable: format!("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {}", user_id)
safe: query!("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1", user_id)

rules.name: OS Command Injection via Shell Execution
rules.description: Detects process command construction where user input is concatenated into shell commands, allowing arbitrary command execution.
rules.detection_strategy:
Detect call_expression to std::process::Command::new or related process spawning functions where an argument contains format! macro or binary_expression with string concatenation.

AST query pattern:
```
(call_expression
  function: (field_expression
    field: (identifier) @field
    field: (identifier) @meth)
  arguments: (arguments
    (macro_invocation macro: (identifier) @macro (#eq? @macro "format!"))))
```

Also detect direct binary_expression concatenation in Command arguments:
```
(call_expression
  function: (field_expression
    object: (type_expression)
    field: (identifier) @field (#eq? @field "new"))
  arguments: (arguments (binary_expression)))
```

Preflight scan for: Command, new, exec, popen, system, sh, bash, cmd, format, std::process
false_positive_guards: Using .arg() method with individual safe arguments
Command arguments are all constant strings
Input validated via whitelist or regex before use
Command construction uses shell = false (no shell interpretation)

rules.name: Path Traversal via User Input in File Operations
rules.description: Detects file path operations where user input is joined or concatenated into paths without proper validation of '../' sequences, allowing access to unauthorized files.
rules.detection_strategy:
Detect call_expression to file system operations (File::open, std::fs::read, std::fs::write, Path::new) where arguments contain format! macro or binary_expression concatenation with user variables.

AST query pattern:
```
(call_expression
  function: (field_expression
    field: (identifier) @field (#any-of? @field "open" "read" "read_to_string" "write" "create"))
  arguments: (arguments
    (macro_invocation macro: (identifier) @macro (#eq? @macro "format!"))))
```

Also detect binary_expression in path construction:
```
(call_expression
  function: (identifier) @func (#any-of? @func "File::open" "std::fs::read" "Path::new"))
  arguments: (arguments (binary_expression)))
```

Preflight scan for: open, read, write, Path, join, canonicalize, from, user, input, file, read_dir
false_positive_guards: Path is canonicalized and checked with starts_with() against base directory
Input validated via whitelist of allowed values

safe: let full = base.join(&user_input).canonicalize()?; if !full.starts_with(base) { return Err(()); }

rules.name: XML External Entity (XXE) Injection
rules.description: Detects XML parser configuration that enables external entity expansion, allowing attackers to read local files or perform SSRF attacks.
rules.detection_strategy:
Detect call_expression to XML parser creation (quick_xml::Reader::new, Reader::from_str, xml::Parser::new) without explicit disabling of external entities.

AST query pattern:
```
(call_expression
  function: (field_expression
    field: (identifier) @field (#any-of? @field "new" "from_str" "from_reader"))
  arguments: (arguments (identifier) @input))

; Check absence of feature disabling in following code
(macro_invocation
  macro: (identifier) @macro (#eq? @macro "with_feature"))
```

Look for Reader creation without subsequent DTD disable:
```
(call_expression
  function: (field_expression
    object: (identifier) @parser (#any-of? @parser "Reader" "Parser"))
  arguments: (arguments))
```

Preflight scan for: XmlReader, Parser, Document, DTD, Entity, external, feature, set, quick_xml, xml
false_positive_guards: External entities explicitly disabled via with_feature()

vulnerable: let mut reader = Reader::new(input);  // without features config

rules.name: Insecure Deserialization of Untrusted Data
rules.description: Detects deserialization of untrusted data using formats known to be unsafe (bincode, MessagePack, YAML), which can lead to arbitrary code execution.
rules.detection_strategy:
Detect call_expression to deserialization methods on unsafe formats (bincode::deserialize, bincode::deserialize_from, serde_yaml::from_str, rmp_serde::from_read, rmp_serde::from_slice) where input is user-controlled.

AST query pattern:
```
(call_expression
  function: (field_expression
    field: (identifier) @method (#any-of? @method "deserialize" "deserialize_from" "from_str" "from_read" "from_slice")))

; Check for unsafe formats in receiver
(field_expression
  object: (identifier) @fmt (#any-of? @fmt "bincode" "serde_yaml" "rmp_serde" "rmp"))
```

Preflight scan for: deserialize, from_slice, from_str, bincode, yaml, serde, MessagePack, rmp, from_read
false_positive_guards: Input from trusted internal source (include_str!, const)
Code is in test module with known data
references: OWASP: A08:2021-Software and Data Integrity Failures

rules.name: LDAP Injection via Filter String Concatenation
rules.description: Detects LDAP query construction where user input is concatenated into filters without proper escaping, allowing filter manipulation.
rules.detection_strategy:
Detect call_expression to ldap search/filter methods where filter argument contains format! macro or string concatenation with user variables.

AST query pattern:
```
(call_expression
  function: (field_expression
    field: (identifier) @field (#any-of? @field "search" "simple_search" "search_filter"))
  arguments: (arguments
    (macro_invocation macro: (identifier) @macro (#eq? @macro "format!"))))
```

Also detect ldap3 crate patterns:
```
(call_expression
  function: (method_identifier)
  arguments: (arguments (binary_expression)))
```

Preflight scan for: ldap, search, filter, find, User, dn, format, ldap3
false_positive_guards: Using filter_escape() or proper LDAP escaping
Validated input against LDAP special characters
sanitizers: Validated against LDAP special chars (*, (, ), \, NUL)

vulnerable: let mut f = String::from("(cn="); f.push_str(&user_input); f.push_str(")")

rules.name: XPath Injection via Expression Concatenation
rules.description: Detects XPath expression construction where user input is concatenated without parameterization, allowing XPath query manipulation.
rules.detection_strategy:
Detect call_expression to XPath evaluation methods (evaluate, select, query) where expression argument contains format! macro or string concatenation.

AST query pattern:
```
(call_expression
  function: (field_expression
    field: (identifier) @field (#any-of? @field "evaluate" "select" "query"))
  arguments: (arguments
    (macro_invocation macro: (identifier) @macro (#eq? @macro "format!"))))
```

Also detect binary_expression concatenation in XPath arguments:
```
(call_expression
  function: (method_identifier)
  arguments: (arguments (binary_expression)))
```

Preflight scan for: xpath, evaluate, query, select, Node, Document, format, xpath
sanitizers: XPath prepared statements with parameter binding
Input escaping for XPath special chars

vulnerable: let expr = format!("//user[@name='{}']", user_name); xpath.evaluate(&expr, document)?

rules.name: Cross-Site Scripting via Unsanitized Output
rules.description: Detects HTML/JS output to web responses where user input is rendered without sanitization, allowing XSS attacks.
rules.detection_strategy:
Detect call_expression to HTTP response methods where body/content contains format! macro interpolating user variables into HTML/JavaScript context.

AST query pattern:
```
(call_expression
  function: (field_expression
    field: (identifier) @field (#any-of? @field "body" "html" "content" "render"))
  arguments: (arguments
    (macro_invocation macro: (identifier) @macro (#eq? @macro "format!")
      arguments: (token_tree) @args)))
```

Detect format! into HTML context:
```
(macro_invocation
  macro: (identifier) @macro (#eq? @macro "format!")
  arguments: (token_tree (string_literal) @lit (#contains? @lit "<")))
```

Preflight scan for: Html, render, response, body, content, format, json, html, HttpResponse, HttpRequest

rules.name: Path Equivalence Without Verification
rules.description: Detects path normalization operations (canonicalize, components, normalize) that don't verify the resulting path is within expected boundaries.
rules.detection_strategy:
Detect call_expression to path normalization methods without subsequent starts_with() or equality check against base path.

AST query pattern:
```
(call_expression
  function: (field_expression
    field: (identifier) @field (#any-of? @field "canonicalize" "components" "normalize"))
  arguments: (arguments (identifier) @input))

; Check for absence of starts_with check
(macro_invocation
  macro: (identifier) @macro (#eq? @macro "if")
  arguments: (token_tree (binary_expression
    field: (identifier) @check (#eq? @check "starts_with"))))
```

Preflight scan for: canonicalize, components, normalize, join, Path, path
false_positive_guards: Canonicalized path checked with starts_with() against base

safe: let p = base.join(&input).canonicalize()?; if !p.starts_with(base) { return Err(()); }

rules.name: Open Redirect via Unvalidated URL
rules.description: Detects HTTP redirects where the destination URL is constructed from user input without validation, allowing attackers to redirect to malicious sites.
rules.detection_strategy:
Detect call_expression to redirect methods (redirect, Location header, MovedPermanently, Found) where URL argument contains format! macro or user input.

AST query pattern:
```
(call_expression
  function: (field_expression
    field: (identifier) @field (#any-of? @field "redirect" "Location" "MovedPermanently" "Found" "SeeOther"))
  arguments: (arguments
    (macro_invocation macro: (identifier) @macro (#eq? @macro "format!"))))
```

Detect insert_header with Location containing user input:
```
(call_expression
  function: (method_identifier)
  arguments: (arguments
    (string_literal) @lit (#contains? @lit "Location")
    (macro_invocation)))
```

Preflight scan for: redirect, location, to_url, Moved, Found, SeeOther, Permanent, format, HttpResponse

rules.name: HTTP Response Splitting via Header Injection
rules.description: Detects HTTP header construction where user input could inject CRLF characters to split responses, enabling cache poisoning or XSS.
rules.detection_strategy:
Detect call_expression to header insertion methods where header value contains format! macro or raw user input without CRLF sanitization.

AST query pattern:
```
(call_expression
  function: (field_expression
    field: (identifier) @field (#any-of? @field "insert_header" "append_header"))
  arguments: (arguments
    (macro_invocation macro: (identifier) @macro (#eq? @macro "format!"))))
```

Detect raw identifier in header value:
```
(call_expression
  function: (method_identifier)
  arguments: (arguments (identifier) @val))
```

Preflight scan for: header, insert_header, append, Content, format, user, input, Set-Cookie

rules.name: Integer Overflow in Size Calculation
rules.description: Detects arithmetic on size/length values where user-controlled multiplication or addition could overflow, leading to buffer overflows or memory issues.
rules.detection_strategy:
Detect binary_expression with arithmetic operators (*, +, -) on size/length values where at least one operand is user-controlled, used in memory allocation context.

AST query pattern:
```
(call_expression
  function: (identifier) @func (#any-of? @func "vec!" "Vec::with_capacity" "Vec::new" "Vec::resize"))
  arguments: (arguments
    (binary_expression
      operator: (operator) @op (#any-of? @op "*" "+" "-")
      left: (field_expression field: (identifier) @len (#eq? @len "len"))
      right: (identifier) @user_val)))
```

Detect vec! macro with multiplication:
```
(macro_invocation
  macro: (identifier) @macro (#eq? @macro "vec!")
  arguments: (token_tree
    (binary_expression operator: (operator) @op (#any-of? @op "*" "+"))))
```

Preflight scan for: len, size, capacity, Vec, vec, new, with_capacity, read, malloc, allocate
false_positive_guards: Input validated to be within safe range

rules.name: Missing Input Sanitization on Public Interface
rules.description: Detects function parameters that accept user input without any visible validation or sanitization before being used in sensitive operations.
rules.detection_strategy:
Detect function definitions with parameters that flow to sensitive operations (database queries, file I/O, process execution) without prior validation calls.

AST query pattern:
```
(function_item
  parameters: (parameters (parameter (identifier) @param))
  body: (block
    (expression_statement
      (call_expression
        function: (identifier) @sink (#any-of? @sink "execute" "query" "open" "Command::new")))))
```

Look for validation patterns before sink:
```
(call_expression
  function: (identifier) @fn (#eq? @fn "validate")
  arguments: (arguments (identifier) @param))
```

Preflight scan for: fn, pub, user, input, request, query, param, validate
false_positive_guards: Validation function called before sensitive operation
Newtype pattern with validation in constructor
sanitizers: Result type with early return on invalid input
references: SonarQube: S5122 (General Violation of Input Validation)

vulnerable: fn new_user(name: String, email: String) -> User { User { name, email } }
safe: pub fn process(data: String) -> Result<()> { validate_input(&data)?; store(&data) }
struct UserInput(String); impl UserInput { pub fn new(s: String) -> Result<Self> { validate(&s)?; Ok(UserInput(s)) } }

rules.name: Unvalidated URL Scheme Allows Dangerous Protocols
rules.description: Detects URL construction where the scheme is not validated, allowing http:// or ftp:// when only https:// should be allowed.
rules.cwe_title: Inclusion of Functionality from Untrusted Control Sphere
rules.detection_strategy:
Detect call_expression to URL parsing methods (Url::parse, reqwest::get) where user input is used without scheme validation.

AST query pattern:
```
(call_expression
  function: (field_expression
    field: (identifier) @method (#eq? @method "parse"))
  arguments: (arguments (identifier) @user_input))

; Check for absence of scheme validation
(binary_expression
  field: (identifier) @field (#eq? @field "scheme"))
```

Detect format! constructing URL with user scheme:
```
(macro_invocation
  macro: (identifier) @macro (#eq? @macro "format!")
  arguments: (token_tree (string_literal) @lit (#contains? @lit "://")))
```

Preflight scan for: Url, parse, url, scheme, http, https, ftp, format, reqwest

vulnerable: let url = format!("{}://{}", user_scheme, user_host); reqwest::get(&url)?
safe: let url = Url::parse(&user_url)?; if url.scheme() != "https" { return Err(()); }

research_notes: Based on CWE (MITRE) - public domain security weakness descriptions
SonarQube security rules: S3649 (SQL Injection), S5131 (XSS), S2076/S2077 (Injection), S4721 (Command Injection)
CodeQL security queries for taint analysis patterns
OWASP Top 10 2021 - A01 (Broken Access Control), A03 (Injection), A05 (Security Misconfiguration), A08 (Software Integrity)
Detection strategies use tree-sitter Rust grammar for AST analysis
All concepts are reference-only from public specifications
